episodes.title: The LEGO Scandal With a Cult, Police Corruption, and a 'Mormon Mafia'
episodes.description:
What started as one man's plan to sell his Lego collection and put his grandkids through college spiraled into one of the strangest internet scandals in recent memory. The story is complete with a corporate store takeover, a "We Steal From Old People" picket campaign, a YouTuber fleeing to Mexico, a SWAT raid, and accusations of a "Mormon Mafia."

An 83-year-old spent 15 years building a collection of 780 LEGO sets and over 1,200 minifigures, then handed it to his son Brian to sell on consignment through a Bricks & Minifigs franchise in Oregon. Then corporate took over the store overnight, and the collection seemingly vanished into a maze of denials, lawsuits, and police involvement.

episodes.title: We Never Stopped Making Cults...
episodes.description:
Join the Gump Club! https://gump.club/ Exclusive content, Discord, and more.
Try our candy! https://sour.gg/ Made in the USA with unique flavors every month.
Play Legend Of Zalk! https://apps.apple.com/us/app/legend-of-zalk/id6745826740

In Today's Episode:
America was founded by men who thought organized religion kept mankind in the shackles of superstition and ignorance... the most rational nation on earth, built in the Age of Reason. So how did a single generation turn it into doomsday prophets, free love communes, eugenics ledgers, and a woman who declared herself the second coming of Christ? One cult poisoned a man and walked. One bred its own members like livestock and became a silverware company you've probably eaten off of. And the servant who blew the whole thing open in court walked out and became Sojourner Truth.

episodes.title: The Epstein Property Nobody Talks About...
episodes.description:
In 1993, a Wall Street financier bought 7,600 acres of New Mexico desert from the governor's family. He built a 26,000 square foot mansion, a private airstrip, and a building his staff called the ladies residence... then told scientists at Harvard and MIT he wanted to use the compound to impregnate 20 women at a time and seed the human race with his DNA. The FBI raided every property Jeffrey Epstein owned. His Manhattan townhouse. His Palm Beach mansion. His private island. But they never searched the ranch. Two tips sat in FBI files for six years. A retired cop reported a concealed incinerator inside a barn. Congress had to force the files out. In March 2026, law enforcement finally searched the property for the first time... seven years after his arrest...

episodes.title: The Files Have Exposed The Cult...
episodes.description:
Exposing the  biggest cult of modern day America. The January 30th release of the Epstein Files; millions of pages, images, and videos the government says are “transparent,” while still withholding key evidence. What was released? What was excluded? Why omissions matter more than what we’re allowed to see...
